item.title: Microchip: Supervisor- Planning
item.description:
locations
AZ - Chandler
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
job requisition id
R1315-25
Are you looking for a unique opportunity to be a part of something great? Want to join a 17,000-member team that works on the technology that powers the world around us? Looking for an atmosphere of trust, empowerment, respect, diversity, and communication? How about an opportunity to own a piece of a multi-billion dollar (with a B!) global organization? We offer all that and more at Microchip Technology Inc.
People come to work at Microchip because we help design the technology that runs the world. They stay because our culture supports their growth and stability. They are challenged and driven by an incredible array of products and solutions with unlimited career potential. Microchip’s nationally-recognized Leadership Passage Programs support career growth where we proudly enroll over a thousand people annually. We take pride in our commitment to employee development, values-based decision making, and strong sense of community, driven by our Vision, Mission, and 11 Guiding Values; we affectionately refer to it as the Aggregate System and it’s won us countless awards for diversity and workplace excellence.
Our company is built by dedicated team players who love to challenge the status quo; we did not achieve record revenue and over 30 years of quarterly profitability without a great team dedicated to empowering innovation. People like you.
Visit our careers page to see what exciting opportunities and company perks await!
Job Description:
The Automotive Product Group (APG) is a vertical marketing organization within Microchip that supports all Business Units (BUs) to drive and strengthen the company’s engagement in the automotive market. The group ensures products, systems, and business processes are prepared to meet the unique requirements of automotive customers.
This role is for an APG Supervisor of Engineering Planning, stationed in Chandler, AZ. The position requires a solution-driven, detail-oriented professional who thrives in a company-wide network, collaborating across BUs, regions, and departments. Success in this role depends on strong data management, process improvement, communication, and leadership skills.
Key Responsibilities
Management
Lead a team of 3 engineers in MPHIL with direct supervisory responsibilities.
Conduct weekly sync calls with global team members.
Manage team planning KPIs, approve time cards and time-off requests.
Serve as backup for the weekly report to senior management.
Act as the primary point of contact for PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) inquiries, collaborating with Product Engineering to resolve issues and enhance efficiency.
Systems & Process Enhancements
Partner with IT to identify system improvements and ensure seamless operation of the Automotive Planning Request process.
Develop and implement business logic for the PPAP portal to improve usability, workflow, and process adherence.
Data Management, PPAP Management & Compliance
Own and maintain high-quality, accurate data related to automotive parts (VAO, Vxx, QTP, OEM SAP, 510 ISELED, SW MCAL).
Collaborate with the New Product Planning group to align product setup with automotive standards (e.g., probe steps, mask patterns).
Track and escalate ongoing PDC issues to resolution.
Work closely with APG Product Engineering on ARA completion prior to automotive release.
Support smooth mask transitions for Automotive RTP or NREL activities.
Ensure quality documents are properly stored, dated, and retrievable in compliance with IATF 16949 requirements.
Maintain accuracy for all PPAP releases, ensuring validity of documents.
Assist internal stakeholders with retrieving and interpreting PPAP data.
Develop and maintain Tableau dashboards and reports to support APG’s analytical requirements across planning, inventory, PPAP, and release compliance.
Pricing & Financial Support
Apply pricing rules for automotive-grade products (e.g., 10% adder to standard-grade devices or BU-specific pricing curves).
Manage and respond to PL-10 pricing inquiries and ensure monthly updates are submitted to Barbara Alvarez.
Change Control and WIP & Inventory Oversight
Review and analyze corporate PCNs (Product Change Notices).
Lead weekly internal team reviews to update APR and ensure timely updates for VAO/APG-Std. devices.
Oversee WIP/FG conversion and manage back-end work orders to support customer demand.
Manage upgrade/downgrade of QS lots to meet allocation needs.
Personal & Professional Skills
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills across all levels (sales, product engineering, operations, marketing, executive management).
High accuracy and attention to data integrity.
Ability to design and conduct training sessions, fostering cross-functional understanding of processes.
Strong problem-solving and solution-oriented mindset.
Capability to manage complex global interactions across multiple BUs, regions, and functions.
Requirements/Qualifications:
Qualifications & Experience
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business, Supply Chain, or related field with 5-6 years of relevant experience, or a Masters degree in Engineering, Business, Supply Chain, or related field with 2.5-3 years of relevant experience.
Proven experience in the automotive semiconductor industry or adjacent sectors.
Prior leadership/supervisory experience managing small teams is preferred.
Experience with data systems (ERP/SAP, Tableau, planning processes).
Strong organizational and project management skills.
This position is not eligible for Microchip immigration sponsorship.
Travel Time:
0% - 25%
Physical Attributes:
Seeing, Talking, Works Alone, Works Around Others
Physical Requirements:
80% sitting, 10% walking, 10% standing
Microchip Technology Inc is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected Veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law.

For more information on applicable equal employment regulations, please refer to the Know Your Rights: Workplace Discrimination is Illegal Poster.
To all recruitment agencies: Microchip Technology Inc. does not accept unsolicited agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our recruiting team or other Microchip employees. Microchip is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

item.title: Microchip: Ingénieur Principal – Analogique/Mixte (Responsable Technique IP) / Principal Engineer - Analog/Mixed-Signal (IP Technical Lead)
item.description:
locations
Canada - Montreal
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
job requisition id
R1571-25
Are you looking for a unique opportunity to be a part of something great? Want to join a 17,000-member team that works on the technology that powers the world around us? Looking for an atmosphere of trust, empowerment, respect, diversity, and communication? How about an opportunity to own a piece of a multi-billion dollar (with a B!) global organization? We offer all that and more at Microchip Technology Inc.
People come to work at Microchip because we help design the technology that runs the world. They stay because our culture supports their growth and stability. They are challenged and driven by an incredible array of products and solutions with unlimited career potential. Microchip’s nationally-recognized Leadership Passage Programs support career growth where we proudly enroll over a thousand people annually. We take pride in our commitment to employee development, values-based decision making, and strong sense of community, driven by our Vision, Mission, and 11 Guiding Values; we affectionately refer to it as the Aggregate System and it’s won us countless awards for diversity and workplace excellence.
Our company is built by dedicated team players who love to challenge the status quo; we did not achieve record revenue and over 30 years of quarterly profitability without a great team dedicated to empowering innovation. People like you.
Visit our careers page to see what exciting opportunities and company perks await!
Job Description:
Description du poste
Le groupe de développement Mixed Signal est responsable de la livraison d’IP analogiques et mixtes aux divisions produits de Microchip. Nous travaillons avec des procédés CMOS de pointe pour produire des circuits intégrés analogiques destinés aux applications filaires. Nous permettons la mise en œuvre de technologies qui permettent aux produits Microchip d’interagir avec le monde extérieur.
En tant que membre du groupe de développement Mixed Signal, le candidat participera au support et au débogage des IP mixtes, notamment des PLL, des SERDES haute vitesse, des CAN, des CNA et d’autres blocs de base analogiques.
Responsabilités
• Définir les exigences pour les IP mixtes en collaboration avec les architectes SoC
• Collaborer avec les fournisseurs d’IP pour personnaliser les solutions IP et finaliser le cahier des charges (SOW)
• Gérer les processus d’intégration des IP, y compris la pré-licence, l’assurance qualité post-acquisition, l’intégration et les revues de validation
• Assurer le support des IP mixtes après la phase de tape-out, y compris les tests en laboratoire, la mise en service client et le débogage
• Participation active aux tests en laboratoire, au support et au débogage des blocs mixtes et SERDES
• Prise en charge des spécifications des circuits et des systèmes
• Concevoir des circuits intégrés analogiques et mixtes en technologie CMOS
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Job Description
The Mixed Signal Development Group is responsible for delivering analog and Mixed-Signal IPs to product divisions within Microchip. We work with leading edge CMOS processes to produce analog integrated circuits for wireline applications. We enable technology that allows Microchip’s products to interface to the outside world.
As a member of the Mixed-Signal Development Group, the candidate will be engaged in the mixed-signal IP support and debug of PLLs, high speed SERDES, ADCs, DACs, and other analog building blocks.
Responsibilities
Define the requirements for Mixed-Signal IPs in discussion with the SoC architects
Collaborate with IP vendors to customize IP solutions and finalize Statement of Work (SOW)
Manage IP intake processes, including pre-licensing and post-acquisition QA, integration, and sign-off reviews
Support Mixed-Signal IP through post-tape out phase, including lab testing, customer bring-up and debug
Active involvement in lab testing, support and debug of mixed-signal and SERDES blocks
Ownership of circuit and system specifications
Design CMOS analog and mixed-signal integrated circuits
Requirements/Qualifications:
Diplôme de licence en génie électrique ou dans un domaine connexe, avec une solide expérience en analogique/mixtes et au moins 10 ans d’expérience en conception de circuits CMOS mixtes, ou un master avec au moins 7,5 ans d’expérience dans ce domaine.
• Excellente compréhension des étapes du flux de conception analogique mixte, y compris l’architecture, la conception et l’intégration
• Expérience avec les circuits SERDES haute vitesse et/ou PLL
• Expérience avec les simulateurs de circuits (HSPICE, Spectre, etc.)
• Une expérience avec les protocoles PCIe, SAS, SATA et Ethernet est un atout
• Une expérience avec les technologies chiplet est un atout
• Bonne compréhension de la physique des dispositifs et des concepts de base ESD/Latch-up
• Excellentes compétences en communication écrite et orale
• Capacité à travailler en équipe
• Esprit innovant et créatif
• Capacité à s’adapter rapidement à de nouveaux projets
Maîtrise de l’anglais (à l’écrit et à l’oral) requise pour ce poste. Les candidats doivent être capables de communiquer efficacement en anglais avec les collègues, les clients et les parties prenantes, ainsi que de lire et comprendre les documents et instructions liés au travail.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering or related field with strong analog/mixed-signal background and at least 10 years of mixed-signal CMOS circuit design experience, or a Master’s degree with at least 7.5 years of such experience.
Deep understanding of the mixed-signal analog design flow steps including architecture, design, and integration
Experience with high-speed SERDES and/or PLL circuits
Experience with circuit simulators (HSPICE, Spectre, etc.)
Experience working with PCIe, SAS, SATA and Ethernet protocols is a plus
Experience working with chiplet technologies is a plus
Possess understanding of device physics and basic ESD/Latch-up concepts
Strong written and oral communication skills
Ability to work in a team environment
Innovative and creative thinker
Ability to quickly ramp on new projects
Travel Time:
0% - 25%
Pay Range:
We offer a total compensation package that ranks among the best in the industry. It consists of competitive base pay, restricted stock units, and quarterly bonus payments. In addition to these components, our package includes health benefits that begin day one, retirement savings plans, and an industry leading IESPP program with a 6-month look back feature. Find more information about all our benefits at the link below:
Benefits of working at Microchip
The annual base salary range for this position is $86,000 - $186,000.*
*Range is dependent on numerous factors including job location, skills and experience.
Ontario Accommodation:
Your accessibility is important to us. If you would like to contact us about our website or need help completing the application process, please email us at CanadaHR@Microchip.com.
In accordance with applicable laws (including human rights and accessibility legislation in Ontario), accommodation will be provided in all parts of the hiring process. Let us know what type of accommodations you require to help remove barriers so that you can participate throughout the interview process.
This contact information is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.
To all recruitment agencies: Microchip Technology Inc. does not accept unsolicited agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our recruiting team or other Microchip employees. Microchip is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

item.title: Analog: Staff Robotics Lab Engineer
item.description:
locations
US, CA, San Jose, Rio Robles
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
job requisition id
R244584
About Analog Devices
Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X).
Analog Devices (ADI) is launching an exciting new initiative — the Emerging Tech Hub — a collaborative space where customers, partners, and academic collaborators can rapidly prototype solutions tailored to their unique challenges.
We are seeking a Staff Robotics Lab Engineer to lead the development of both robotic software frameworks and high-performance mechanical infrastructure in support of ADI's cutting-edge AI and robotics innovations. This hybrid role combines deep hands-on expertise in mechatronics, system integration, and lab operations with the ability to design, build, and support robotic solutions from the ground up.
You will work closely with customers, researchers, and product development teams to advance intelligent robotic systems from concept to working prototypes — driving both internal engineering excellence and exceptional customer-facing engagement.
Key Responsibilities
Customer-Facing Technical Leadership
Serve as the technical point of contact for customer and partner engagements at the Emerging Tech Hub.
Translate customer application requirements into integrated robotics solutions, including mechanical, electrical, and software components.
Lead technical demonstrations, proof-of-concept builds, and rapid iterations based on user feedback.
Robotic Software & Framework Development
Develop and maintain core robotic middleware frameworks (e.g., ROS2), sensor interfaces, and test automation scripts in Python and C++.
Collaborate with software engineers and researchers to enable intelligent perception, motion planning, and AI integration in robotic platforms.
Build validation environments and support simulation/hardware-in-the-loop testing.
Mechanical & Systems Engineering
Design, assemble, and integrate custom robotic testbeds, sensor mounts, motion platforms, and modular tooling.
Support mechanical prototyping via 3D printing (FDM/SLA), CNC machining, and CAD design (SolidWorks/Fusion 360).
Perform system-level debugging and precision calibration for electro-mechanical assemblies.
Advanced Lab Management
Lead day-to-day robotics lab operations including equipment upkeep, workspace layout, and safety compliance.
Interface with Facilities and EH&S to uphold safety protocols and support lab audits.
Ensure the lab remains a functional, efficient, and innovation-driven space for rapid development.
Inventory & Vendor Coordination
Manage lab inventory including sensors, robotic parts, mechanical components, and consumables.
Evaluate vendors, oversee purchases, and coordinate equipment installations and service agreements.
Qualifications
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Robotics, Mechatronics, Electrical Engineering, or related discipline.
7+ years of experience in an advanced robotics, prototyping, or R&D lab environment with both mechanical and software responsibilities.
Strong experience in ROS/ROS2, Python, and C++ for robotic system development.
Proven ability to design, build, and test mechanical fixtures, sensor rigs, or actuator systems.
Skilled in soldering, PCB rework, system integration, and electro-mechanical troubleshooting.
Proficiency in CAD tools (SolidWorks, Fusion 360), 3D printing, and machine shop tools.
Comfortable working directly with customers and partners to co-develop solutions and communicate technical decisions.
Familiarity with lab management systems, safety standards, and documentation practices.
Bonus: Experience with mobile robots, collaborative arms, or real-time control systems.
For positions requiring access to technical data, Analog Devices, Inc. may have to obtain export licensing approval from the U.S. Department of Commerce - Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the U.S. Department of State - Directorate of Defense Trade Controls. As such, applicants for this position – except US Citizens, US Permanent Residents, and protected individuals as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3) – may have to go through an export licensing review process.
Analog Devices is an equal opportunity employer. We foster a culture where everyone has an opportunity to succeed regardless of their race, color, religion, age, ancestry, national origin, social or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, military or veteran status, union membership, and political affiliation, or any other legally protected group.
EEO is the Law: Notice of Applicant Rights Under the Law.
Job Req Type: Experienced
Required Travel: Yes, 25% of the time
Shift Type: 1st Shift/Days
The expected wage range for a new hire into this position is $144,038 to $216,056.
Actual wage offered may vary depending on work location, experience, education, training, external market data, internal pay equity, or other bona fide factors.
This position qualifies for a discretionary performance-based bonus which is based on personal and company factors.
This position includes medical, vision and dental coverage, 401k, paid vacation, holidays, and sick time, and other benefits.

item.title: Analog: Staff Engineer, Systems Integration Engineering
item.description:
locations
US, MA, Wilmington
time type
Full time
posted on
Posted Today
job requisition id
R251483
About Analog Devices
Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X).
Staff Electronic Systems Design Engineer – Energy
The Data Center and Energy BU's purpose is to engineer a decarbonized, cleaner, and a more sustainable electrified future, and drive efficiencies in data centers. Developing solutions for the digitized grid; renewable energy generation; energy transmission, distribution, and storage; EV charging infrastructure; smart meters; and energy conversion, the group supports customers with break-through technology to convert, manage, and store electrical power.
We have immediate openings for key positions within the group.
The Role
The Staff Electronic Systems Design Engineer will lead the design and delivery of innovative system solutions to customers in the electric energy ecosystem, from the modernization of the transmission and distribution grid to green energy generation, and electric power conversion to energy storage systems. Product types include advanced grid sensors, smart meters, photovoltaic (PV) inverters, EV chargers, and ACDC and DCDC power supplies. The role requires a flexible and highly skilled systems engineer with expertise in electronic system and applications design while possessing strong communication and interpersonal skills. The candidate should relish finding elegant solutions to complex system level challenges. Responsibilities include
Develop electrical energy systems from concept, through architecture and design to production.
Identify and derive requirements from customer needs through to system requirements.
Contribute to the architecture based on requirements (appropriate systems modelling and capturing into a requirements management tool) and coordinate it with the customer and internal stakeholders including software, hardware, security, and functional safety.
Work with technology experts in related Conduct design reviews with project team.
Design, build, and test electric energy products
Achieve appropriate industry certifications on products
Work with marketing on product strategy planning and customer engagement.
Opportunities exist to lead projects or a team to accomplish group goals.
Requirements
The candidate should have a firm grasp of hardware design principles and also understand embedded software development and control loop design for electric energy systems. Strong candidates will have many of the following experience and qualifications:
Minimum BS in Electrical Engineering; MSEE and 8+ years of experience preferred.
The candidate is expected to employ systems thinking and have demonstrated excellence in systems design in one or more electric energy industries within the last 5-10 years.
Experience in designing electronics hardware for one or more of the following, from high level design to detailed circuit design, and bring-up testing, characterization, and certification to industry standards
high voltage (up to 2 kV) energy systems including 40-200 kW power levels
renewable energy generation and connection to the grid, including sensors and energy metering
data center power supply design
battery energy storage systems and/or battery management systems
electronics design in related industries such as industrial robotics, consumer electronics, and high-reliability systems
Experience in electrical circuit design, cooling systems, and software algorithms and control.
Experience in functional safety designs is desired.
Proven experience in full system design with good knowledge of systems modelling and requirements capture, especially in the context of safety-relevant systems.
Knowledge of certification requirements and procedures in relevant countries is desired
Familiarity with a variety of lab test equipment and also tools like JAMA and Matlab.
Strong candidates should exhibit or possess the following attributes:
Experience with articulating and presenting concepts to customers and uncovering their needs.
Strong problem-solving skills with an ability to understand and clearly articulate technical issues.
Demonstrated team player, excellent communication, and leadership skills.
Enjoys working in a dynamic environment in fast-growing markets, and openly embraces working with others, including across groups, and actively seeks to do so.
Ability to travel for customer and partner meetings as required.
Travel Required: Yes, 5-10% of the Time
Security Clearance Required: No
For positions requiring access to technical data, Analog Devices, Inc. may have to obtain export licensing approval from the U.S. Department of Commerce - Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the U.S. Department of State - Directorate of Defense Trade Controls. As such, applicants for this position – except US Citizens, US Permanent Residents, and protected individuals as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3) – may have to go through an export licensing review process.
Analog Devices is an equal opportunity employer. We foster a culture where everyone has an opportunity to succeed regardless of their race, color, religion, age, ancestry, national origin, social or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, military or veteran status, union membership, and political affiliation, or any other legally protected group.
EEO is the Law: Notice of Applicant Rights Under the Law.
Job Req Type: Experienced
Required Travel: Yes, 10% of the time
Shift Type: 1st Shift/Days
The expected wage range for a new hire into this position is $125,250 to $187,875.
Actual wage offered may vary depending on work location, experience, education, training, external market data, internal pay equity, or other bona fide factors.
This position qualifies for a discretionary performance-based bonus which is based on personal and company factors.
This position includes medical, vision and dental coverage, 401k, paid vacation, holidays, and sick time, and other benefits.
